creator: ehaupt@FreeBSD.org
text:
Just wanted to follow up. From the information you provided, it seems that the issue you're facing may not be specific to FreeBSD, but rather related to the window manager or compositing window manager being used.

In this case, it might be a good idea to reach out to the upstream project responsible for the window manager or the compositing window manager (like compiz or xcompmgr) that you mentioned. They would have a better understanding of the window management system and could provide more insights into the behavior you're encountering.

By engaging with the upstream project, you can raise the issue and ensure that the right developers are aware of the problem. They'll be in a better position to guide you and potentially offer improvements or solutions to overcome the problem.
